revision.comment: 新しいページ：塚原二子塚古墳（長野県飯田市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 塚原二子塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 塚原1号墳 |所属 = [[飯田古墳群]]（竜丘地区）
塚原古墳群 |所在地 = [[長野県]][[飯田市]]桐林3046-1ほか |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 53.50 |経度度 = 137|経度分 = 49|経度秒 = 14.47 |ISO = JP-20 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長73m |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[竪穴式石室]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]]、（伝）金銅製冑 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「飯田古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagano |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南南西 |ラベル = 塚原
二子塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''塚原二子塚古墳'''（つかばらふたごづかこふん）は、[[長野県]][[飯田市]]桐林にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[飯田古墳群]]（うち竜丘地区）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「飯田古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 長野県南部、[[天竜川]]支流の臼井川・袋洞沢川の間の小段丘上（標高405m）に築造された古墳である。一帯にはかつて古墳16基が分布して塚原古墳群を形成し、本古墳はその盟主的存在であったが、現在では本古墳以外の多くは失われている。現在までに良好に墳丘を遺存しており、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）・[[2007年|2007]]-[[2009年]]度（[[平成]]19-21年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南南西方向に向ける。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（蓋形埴輪など）が認められる。また墳丘周囲には盾形の周溝が巡らされ、周溝を含めた古墳全長は86メートルにおよぶ。埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでないが、後円部墳頂における[[竪穴式石室]]と推定される。副葬品も明らかでないが、かつて金銅製冑が出土したという伝承がある{{Sfn|飯田古墳群|2012}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html 塚原二子塚古墳]（飯田市ホームページ）。（または5世紀末史跡説明板。/5世紀後半-末{{Sfn|飯田古墳群|2012}}）頃と推定される。 古墳域は[[2016年]]（平成28年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「飯田古墳群」のうち）。 === 来歴 === * [[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）、耕作時にくびれ部付近で埴輪列の出土{{Sfn|飯田における古墳の出現と展開 資料編|2007}}。 * [[1968年]]（昭和43年）11月19日、飯田市指定史跡に指定。 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）、墳丘測量調査{{Sfn|飯田における古墳の出現と展開 資料編|2007}}。 * [[1990年]]度（[[平成]]2年度）、市道建設に伴う後円部北側の発掘調査（飯田市教育委員会）。 * [[2007年|2007]]-[[2009年]]度（平成19-21年度）、範囲確認調査（飯田市教育委員会、[[2012年]]に報告）{{Sfn|飯田古墳群|2012}}。 * [[2016年]]（平成28年）10月3日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「飯田古墳群」のうち）。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|飯田古墳群|2012}}。 * 古墳総長：86メートル - 周溝を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：73メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：44メートル ** 高さ：6.2メートル（西側）、7.5-7.6メートル（東側） * 前方部 ** 幅：38メートル ** 高さ：6.0-6.2メートル（西側）、7.4メートル（東側） 墳丘外表では葺石が認められるが、通常のように墳丘の傾斜に沿って積み上げるのではなく、階段状（石垣状）に積み上げ、外見的には基壇状を呈する独特の構造として注目される{{Sfn|飯田古墳群|2012}}。 墳丘の周囲には盾形の周溝が巡らされ、後円部側ではその外側にも外周溝が認められる（二重周溝）{{Sfn|飯田古墳群|2012}}。 Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む == ギャラリー == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|内山塚古墳（塚原2号墳、円墳） Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|塚原3号墳（帆立貝形古墳） Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|鏡塚古墳（塚原4号墳、帆立貝形古墳） Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|鎧塚古墳（塚原5号墳、帆立貝形古墳） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（飯田市教育委員会、2017年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=塚原二子塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 20 長野県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|塚原二子塚古墳（平凡社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小林正春|year=2002|chapter=二子塚古墳 > 塚原二子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|塚原二子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=塚原二子塚古墳|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=飯田における古墳の出現と展開 -資料編-|publisher=飯田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|飯田における古墳の出現と展開 資料編|2007}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=塚原二子塚古墳範囲確認調査報告|url=|title=飯田古墳群|publisher=飯田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|飯田古墳群|2012}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html 塚原二子塚古墳] - 飯田市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:つかはらふたこつかこふん}} [[Category:飯田古墳群]] [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:飯田市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長野県にある国指定の史跡]]

墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 塚原1号墳 |所属 = [[飯田古墳群]]（竜丘単位群）
塚原古墳群 |所在地 = [[長野県]][[飯田市]]桐林3046-1ほか |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 27|緯度秒 = 53.50 |経度度 = 137|経度分 = 49|経度秒 = 14.47 |ISO = JP-20 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長73m |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[竪穴式石室]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]]、（伝）金銅製冑 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「飯田古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagano |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南南西 |ラベル = 塚原
二子塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''塚原二子塚古墳'''（つかばらふたごづかこふん）は、[[長野県]][[飯田市]]桐林にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[飯田古墳群]]（うち竜丘単位群）を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「飯田古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == 長野県南部、[[天竜川]]支流の臼井川・袋洞沢川の間の小段丘上（標高405m）に築造された古墳である。一帯にはかつて古墳16基が分布して塚原古墳群を形成し、本古墳はその盟主的存在であったが、現在では本古墳以外の多くは失われている。現在までに良好に墳丘を遺存しており、[[1980年]]（[[昭和]]55年）・[[2007年|2007]]-[[2009年]]度（[[平成]]19-21年度）に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南南西方向に向ける。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]（朝顔形埴輪含む）・[[形象埴輪]]（蓋形埴輪など）が認められる。また墳丘周囲には盾形の周溝が巡らされ、周溝を含めた古墳全長は86メートルにおよぶ。埋葬施設は未調査のため明らかでないが、後円部墳頂における[[竪穴式石室]]と推定される。副葬品も明らかでないが、かつて金銅製冑が出土したという伝承がある{{Sfn|飯田古墳群|2012}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]後半[https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html 塚原二子塚古墳]（飯田市ホームページ）。（または5世紀末史跡説明板。/5世紀後半-末{{Sfn|飯田古墳群|2012}}）頃と推定される。 古墳域は[[2016年]]（平成28年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「飯田古墳群」のうち）。 === 来歴 === * [[1957年]]（[[昭和]]32年）、耕作時にくびれ部付近で埴輪列の出土{{Sfn|飯田における古墳の出現と展開 資料編|2007}}。 * [[1968年]]（昭和43年）11月19日、飯田市指定史跡に指定。 * [[1980年]]（昭和55年）、墳丘測量調査{{Sfn|飯田における古墳の出現と展開 資料編|2007}}。 * [[1990年]]度（[[平成]]2年度）、市道建設に伴う後円部北側の発掘調査（飯田市教育委員会）。 * [[2007年|2007]]-[[2009年]]度（平成19-21年度）、範囲確認調査（飯田市教育委員会、[[2012年]]に報告）{{Sfn|飯田古墳群|2012}}。 * [[2016年]]（平成28年）10月3日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「飯田古墳群」のうち）。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部墳頂}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|飯田古墳群|2012}}。 * 古墳総長：86メートル - 周溝を含めた全長。 * 墳丘長：73メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：44メートル ** 高さ：6.2メートル（西側）、7.5-7.6メートル（東側） * 前方部 ** 幅：38メートル ** 高さ：6.0-6.2メートル（西側）、7.4メートル（東側） 墳丘外表では葺石が認められるが、通常のように墳丘の傾斜に沿って積み上げるのではなく、階段状（石垣状）に積み上げ、外見的には基壇状を呈する独特の構造として注目される{{Sfn|飯田古墳群|2012}}。 墳丘の周囲には盾形の周溝が巡らされ、後円部側ではその外側にも外周溝が認められる（二重周溝）{{Sfn|飯田古墳群|2012}}。 Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪
{{small|飯田市考古博物館展示。}} == ギャラリー == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|内山塚古墳（塚原2号墳、円墳） Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|塚原3号墳（帆立貝形古墳） Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|鏡塚古墳（塚原4号墳、帆立貝形古墳） Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|鎧塚古墳（塚原5号墳、帆立貝形古墳） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（飯田市教育委員会、2017年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1979|chapter=塚原二子塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 20 長野県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|塚原二子塚古墳（平凡社）|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=小林正春|year=2002|chapter=二子塚古墳 > 塚原二子塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|塚原二子塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2007|chapter=塚原二子塚古墳|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=飯田における古墳の出現と展開 -資料編-|publisher=飯田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|飯田における古墳の出現と展開 資料編|2007}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=塚原二子塚古墳範囲確認調査報告|url=|title=飯田古墳群|publisher=飯田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|飯田古墳群|2012}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html 塚原二子塚古墳] - 飯田市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:つかはらふたこつかこふん}} [[Category:飯田古墳群]] [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:飯田市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長野県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.comment: added [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]
revision.text:
塚原二子塚古墳
[[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]]

塚原二子塚古墳 [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |other_name = Tsukabara No.1 Tumulus |part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun Cluster|ja|飯田古墳群}} (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |latitude_deg = 35|latitude_min = 27|latitude_sec = 53.50 |longitude_deg = 137|longitude_min = 49|longitude_sec = 14.47 |ISO = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |length = 73m |burial_chamber = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iida Kofun Cluster" |map = Japan Nagano |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun Cluster|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi (paving stones)|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the shape of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was designated as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun Cluster" in 2016. == Tumulus == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Tumulus Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped platform. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, forming a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Tumulus, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Tumulus (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place Names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - revision.text:
塚原二子塚古墳 [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |other_name = Tsukabara No.1 Tumulus |part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun Cluster|ja|飯田古墳群}} (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |latitude_deg = 35|latitude_min = 27|latitude_sec = 53.50 |longitude_deg = 137|longitude_min = 49|longitude_sec = 14.47 |ISO = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |length = 73m |burial_chamber = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iida Kofun Cluster" |map = Japan Nagano |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun Cluster|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi (paving stones)|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the shape of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was designated as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun Cluster" in 2016. == Tumulus == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Tumulus Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped platform. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, forming a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Tumulus, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Tumulus (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place Names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun Cluster|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun Cluster|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website [[Category:Iida Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped kofun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]]

A moat in the shape of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was designated as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun Cluster" in 2016. == Tumulus == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Tumulus Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped platform. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, forming a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Tumulus, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Tumulus (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place Names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun Cluster|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi (paving stones)|ja|葺石}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the shape of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was designated as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun Cluster" in 2016. == Tumulus == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Tumulus Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped platform. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, forming a double moat structure. Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |latitude_deg = 35|latitude_min = 27|latitude_sec = 53.50 |longitude_deg = 137|longitude_min = 49|longitude_sec = 14.47 |ISO = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |length = 73m |burial_chamber = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the shape of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was designated as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Tumulus == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Tumulus Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped platform. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, forming a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Tumulus, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Tumulus (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place Names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - revision.text:
塚原二子塚古墳 [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |other_name = Tsukabara No.1 Tumulus |part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}} (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |latitude_deg = 35|latitude_min = 27|latitude_sec = 53.50 |longitude_deg = 137|longitude_min = 49|longitude_sec = 14.47 |ISO = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |length = 73m |burial_chamber = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the shape of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was designated as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Tumulus == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Tumulus Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped platform. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, forming a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Tumulus, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Tumulus (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place Names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |latitude_deg = 35|latitude_min = 27|latitude_sec = 53.50 |longitude_deg = 137|longitude_min = 49|longitude_sec = 14.47 |ISO = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |length = 73m |burial_chamber = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the shape of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was designated as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Tumulus == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Tumulus Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped platform. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, forming a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Tumulus, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Tumulus (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place Names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. The kofun was designated as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Tumulus == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Tumulus Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped platform. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, forming a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Tumulus, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Tumulus (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place Names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - 塚原二子塚古墳 [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |other_name = Tsukabara No.1 Tumulus |part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}} (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |latitude_deg = 35|latitude_min = 27|latitude_sec = 53.50 |longitude_deg = 137|longitude_min = 49|longitude_sec = 14.47 |ISO = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} |length = 73m |burial_chamber = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the shape of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was designated as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Tumulus == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Tumulus Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped platform. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, forming a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Tumulus, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Tumulus (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place Names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|史跡}" to "|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}"
revision.text:
塚原二子塚古墳 [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |other_name = Tsukabara No.1 Tumulus |part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}} (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |latitude_deg = 35|latitude_min = 27|latitude_sec = 53.50 |longitude_deg = 137|longitude_min = 49|longitude_sec = 14.47 |ISO = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |length = 73m |burial_chamber = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the shape of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was designated as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Tumulus == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Tumulus Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped platform. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, forming a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Tumulus, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Tumulus (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place Names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.text:
塚原二子塚古墳 [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |other_name = Tsukabara No.1 Tumulus |part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}} (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |latitude_deg = 35|latitude_min = 27|latitude_sec = 53.50 |longitude_deg = 137|longitude_min = 49|longitude_sec = 14.47 |ISO = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |length = 73m |burial_chamber = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the shape of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was designated as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Tumulus == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Tumulus Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped platform. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, forming a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Tumulus, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Tumulus (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Tumulus, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place Names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]] [[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Tumulus" to "Kofun"
revision.text:
塚原二子塚古墳 [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |other_name = Tsukabara No.1 Kofun |part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}} (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |latitude_deg = 35|latitude_min = 27|latitude_sec = 53.50 |longitude_deg = 137|longitude_min = 49|longitude_sec = 14.47 |ISO = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |length = 73m |burial_chamber = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the shape of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was designated as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped platform. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, forming a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place Names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
塚原二子塚古墳 [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |other_name = Tsukabara No.1 Kofun |part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}} (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |latitude_deg = 35|latitude_min = 27|latitude_sec = 53.50 |longitude_deg = 137|longitude_min = 49|longitude_sec = 14.47 |ISO = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |length = 73m |burial_chamber = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the shape of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was designated as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped platform. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, forming a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place Names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]
