Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |length = 73m |burial_chamber = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|other_other_designations of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is other_designation1 as a national {{ill|other_other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was other_designation1 as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - revision.comment: Text replacement - "burial_chamber" to "burial"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} 塚原二子塚古墳 [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |other_name = Tsukabara No.1 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}} (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |length = 73m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|other_designations of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is other_designation1 as a national {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was other_designation1 as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National other_designations of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]]

Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was other_designation1 as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National other_designations of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} 塚原二子塚古墳 [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |other_name = Tsukabara No.1 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}} (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |length = 73m |burial_chamber = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|other_designations of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is other_designation1 as a national {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was other_designation1 as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National other_designations of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} 塚原二子塚古墳 [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |alternate_name = Tsukabara No.1 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}} (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |length = 73m |burial_chamber = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|other_designations of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is other_designation1 as a national {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was other_designation1 as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National other_designations of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is other_designation1 as a national {{ill|other_designation of Japan|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was other_designation1 as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Tier 5]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} 塚原二子塚古墳 [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |alternate_name = Tsukabara No.1 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}} (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |length = 73m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)|12=draft}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]] [[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} 塚原二子塚古墳 {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]

Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市|12=draft}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} |length = 73m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群|12=draft}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)|12=draft}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群|12=draft}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)|12=draft}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}}. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]] [[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]]

Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市|12=draft|12=draft}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |length = 73m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft|12=draft}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]] [[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]]

revision.comment: adding secondary category for the ones still with unknown parameters
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} 塚原二子塚古墳 {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |alternate_name = Tsukabara No.1 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市|12=draft|12=draft}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} |length = 73m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic sites of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|ja|飯田市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|ja|飯田古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site of Japan|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan|12=draft|12=draft}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川|12=draft|12=draft}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:Iida Kofun group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida, Nagano]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National Historic sites of Japan in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[en:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]] [[sn:Tsukahara Futagozuka Kofun]]
