[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q122552499]] Wikidata name: Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} 塚原二子塚古墳 {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |alternate_name = Tsukabara No.1 Kofun | part_of = [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun group]] (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]] Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |length = 73m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun group]], specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]], Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi (paving stones)]] and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names Series|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website {{translated page|ja|塚原二子塚古墳|version=99995773|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] {{Commons category|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q122552499}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun group]], specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]], Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi (paving stones)]] and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q122552499]] Wikidata name: Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} 塚原二子塚古墳 {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |alternate_name = Tsukabara No.1 Kofun | part_of = [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun group]] (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]] Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |length = 73m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun group]], specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]], Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi (paving stones)]] and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names Series|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website {{translated page|ja|塚原二子塚古墳|version=99995773|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] {{Commons category|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q122552499}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q122552499}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]] Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |length = 73m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun group]], specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]], Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi (paving stones)]] and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names Series|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website {{translated page|ja|塚原二子塚古墳|version=99995773|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] {{Commons category|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q122552499}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q122552499}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q122552499]] Wikidata name: Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} 塚原二子塚古墳 {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |alternate_name = Tsukabara No.1 Kofun | part_of = [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun group]] (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]] Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |length = 73m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun group]], specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]], Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi (paving stones)]] and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names Series|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website {{translated page|ja|塚原二子塚古墳|version=99995773|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] {{Commons category|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q122552499}}

Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]] Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |length = 73m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun group]], specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]], Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi (paving stones)]] and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names Series|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website {{translated page|ja|塚原二子塚古墳|version=99995773|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iida]] {{Commons category|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q122552499}}

{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names Series|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[commons:Category:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q122552499]] Wikidata name: Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} 塚原二子塚古墳 {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |alternate_name = Tsukabara No.1 Kofun | part_of = [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun group]] (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]] Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |length = 73m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun group]], specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]], Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi (paving stones)]] and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names Series|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website {{translated page|ja|塚原二子塚古墳|version=99995773|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q122552499}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]] Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |length = 73m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun group]], specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]], Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi (paving stones)]] and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names Series|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website {{translated page|ja|塚原二子塚古墳|version=99995773|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q122552499}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names Series|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - revision.comment: Bot: categorize pages with unclosed brackets
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun]][[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q122552499]] Wikidata name: Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} 塚原二子塚古墳 {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |alternate_name = Tsukabara No.1 Kofun | part_of = [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun group]] (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]] Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |length = 73m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iida, Nagano|Iida, Nagano]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] and part of the [[:en:Iida Kofun group|Iida Kofun group]], specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the [[:en:Tenryū River|Tenryū River]], Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi (paving stones)]] and [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]]. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names Series|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website {{translated page|ja|塚原二子塚古墳|version=99995773|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q122552499}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} 塚原二子塚古墳 {{Kofun of Japan |name = Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right, rear circular part in the back left) |alternate_name = Tsukabara No.1 Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Iida Kofun group|en|Iida Kofun group|WD=Q48745895|lt=Iida Kofun group}} (Ryūkyū Unit Cluster)
Tsukabara Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} {{ill|Iida, Nagano|en|Iida, Nagano|WD=Q841129|lt=Iida, Nagano}} Kiribayashi 3046-1, etc. |coordinates = 35|coordinates = 27|coordinates = 53.50 |coordinates = 137|coordinates = 49|coordinates = 14.47 |omitted = JP-20 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} |length = 73m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}, (traditionally) gilt-bronze helmet |constructed = Late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}} |buried_person = |historic_site = Part of the national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iida Kofun group" |map = Japan Nagano |type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - south-southwest |map_dot_label = Tsukabara
Futagozuka Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun''' (塚原二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iida, Nagano|en|Iida, Nagano|WD=Q841129|lt=Iida, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} and part of the {{ill|Iida Kofun group|en|Iida Kofun group|WD=Q48745895|lt=Iida Kofun group}}, specifically within the Ryūkyū Unit Cluster. The site is Historic site as a national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a small terrace (elevation 405m) between the tributaries of the {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|WD=Q13667|lt=Tenryū River}}, Usui River, and Fukurohorisawa River in southern Nagano Prefecture. It was once part of the Tsukabara Kofun Cluster, which consisted of 16 tumuli, but most have been lost, leaving this kofun as the dominant structure. Excavations were conducted in 1980 and again from 2007 to 2009. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped, with the front part facing south-southwest. The mound surface shows traces of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi (paving stones)}} and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} types, including cylindrical and figurative haniwa. A moat in the type of a shield surrounds the mound, bringing the total length of the kofun to 86 meters. The burial chamber has not been excavated, but it is presumed to be a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} at the top of the rear circular mound. While burial goods are unclear, there is a tradition that a gilt-bronze helmet was unearthed from the site. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century}}. The kofun was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Iida Kofun group" in 2016. == Kofun == [[File:Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Rear Circular Mound Top}}]] The area of the mound are as follows: * Total Length: 86 meters (including the surrounding moat) * Kofun Length: 73 meters * Rear Circular Mound ** Diameter: 44 meters ** Height: 6.2 meters (west side), 7.5-7.6 meters (east side) * Front Part ** Width: 38 meters ** Height: 6.0-6.2 meters (west side), 7.4 meters (east side) Unlike typical kofun where fukiishi are laid along the slope, here the stones are stacked in a terraced or stone-wall-like manner, giving the appearance of a stepped plattype. The moat is shield-shaped with an additional outer moat on the rear circular side, typeing a double moat structure. Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular mound Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular mound toward the front part 塚原二子塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Displayed at Iida City Archaeological Museum.}} == Gallery == Uchiyamazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|Uchiyamazuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.2 Kofun, round kofun) Tsukabara Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Tsukabara No.3 Kofun (scallop-shaped kofun) Kagamizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Kagamizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.4 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) Yoroizuka Kofun (Iida), funkyu.jpg|Yoroizuka Kofun (Tsukabara No.5 Kofun, scallop-shaped kofun) == References == {{reflist}} == Sources == * Explanatory plaque (Iida City Board of Education, installed in 2017) * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=1979|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japan Historical Place names Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Historical Place names Series|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 20 Nagano Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490204|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1979}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=Masaharu Kobayashi|year=2002|chapter=Futagozuka Kofun > Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Continued {{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2007|chapter=Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9271 |title=The Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida - Resource Edition|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Emergence and Development of Kofun in Iida Resource Edition|2007}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|author=|year=2012|chapter=Survey Report on Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun|title=Iida Kofun group|publisher=Iida City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Iida Kofun group|2012}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/futagoduka.html Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun] – Iida City Official Website {{translated page|ja|塚原二子塚古墳|version=99995773|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:塚原二子塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Tsukabara Futagozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q122552499}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Iida]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagano prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]
